rationale: Major friction: this is a live-action UGC dance reaction tied to a word game (Crossword Go), with an earthy muted palette and a "post-school all-nighter" framing that skews younger/student — misaligned with Block Blast's 25-55 casual relaxer audience and its glossy vibrant blue/gold abstract look. The hook also shows zero gameplay, so it fails to communicate the spatial drag-and-drop line-clear loop that drives Block Blast installs. Only worth testing if reshot with a 25-45 talent, swapped overlay to "how I feel after clearing a 4-line combo," and a picture-in-picture or cutaway to actual 8x8 grid gameplay; otherwise skip — cluster is also tiny (n=1) and unproven.
